article_title: A multi-centre stereotactic radiosurgery planning study of multiple brain metastases using isocentric linear accelerators with 5 and 2.5 mm width multi-leaf collimators, CyberKnife and Gamma Knife
label: Figure 1.
caption: Five arc beam arrangement used in Elements Multiple Brain Mets SRS planning. OARs shown are left eye (red), right eye (green), optic nerve and optic tracts (orange), optic chiasm (yellow), brainstem (green), hippocampi (blue), and cerebellum (brown). Ten targets are shown in various colours throughout the arc arrangements.

article_title: A multi-centre stereotactic radiosurgery planning study of multiple brain metastases using isocentric linear accelerators with 5 and 2.5 mm width multi-leaf collimators, CyberKnife and Gamma Knife
label: Figure 2.
caption: Dose distribution for (A) linac with HD MLCs and (B) standard MLCs; (C) CK and (D) GK for a patient with 5 targets all treated to 24 Gy.

article_title: A multi-centre stereotactic radiosurgery planning study of multiple brain metastases using isocentric linear accelerators with 5 and 2.5 mm width multi-leaf collimators, CyberKnife and Gamma Knife
label: Figure 3.
caption: Cumulative DVHs of the five PTVs all prescribed to 24 Gy and the normal whole brain for a selected patient comparing the linac using HD MLCs with (A) standard MLCs, (B) CK, and (C) GK. Abbreviations: WB = normal whole brain, DVH = dose-volume histogram, HD MLC = linac using high-definition MLCs; MLC = linac using standard MLCs, CK = CyberKnife, GK = Gamma Knife.
